Trait_def: How recognizable is the name and face? Not all fame is good, but this is purely recognition, not brilliance or popularity.
Trait_rating_notes: People behind the scenes are less likely to be famous even if they are brilliant at what they do. Some Showrunners qualify with elite fame, some executive producers might be 'great' level. Writers are unlikely to have more than average fame; being highly regarded in the industry is not the same as Fame here
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Tom Cruise (99), Bradley Cooper (85), Florence Pugh (75), Josh O'Connor (60), Asher Book (35), Anderson Cooper (98), Megyn Kelly (87), David Muir (79), Alex Witt (65), Evrod Cassimy (35), Steven Spielberg (99), Shonda Rhimes (85), David E. Kelley (75), John Hoffman (55), Julie M. Anderson (35)

Trait_def: How traditionally handsome or beautiful is the person?
Trait_rating_notes: A person behind the scenes can possess this, but it's more impactful for those on screen. The standard here is higher than for the average person. Even average attractiveness would be well above average in the regular population. Also, it's harder to have a high score in older ages. Only a few people manage to seriously maintain elite attractiveness past 50 -- George Clooney, Pierce Brosnan
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Margot Robbie (98), Ryan Gosling (88), Cillian Murphy (78), John C. Reilly (55), Clint Howard (35), Abby Phillip (94), David Muir (89), Anderson Cooper (79), Wolf Blitzer (55), Keith Olbermann (35), Greta Gerwig (93), Sofia Coppola (85), Christopher Nolan (75), Judd Apatow (50), George R.R. Martin (35)

Trait_def: The raw magnetic authority and "weight" a person carries on screen or in a room. It is the measure of how much an audience is naturally compelled to watch them.
Trait_rating_notes: Actors are measured by this metric directly; Anchors, reporters, reality hosts can also possess this skill but on average, won't be as proficient; Off camera talent, like producers, writers, and showrunners would not possess this skill typically, except where some have demonstrated crossover ability on screen.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Meryl Streep (99), Tom Hanks (88), Idris Elba (78), West Mulholland (65), Eddy Maday (35), Anderson Cooper (98), Christiane Amanpour (89), Lester Holt (79), Alex Witt (68), Evrod Cassimy (35), Steven Soderbergh (95), Shonda Rhimes (85), David Koepp (75), Zack Ryan (50), Julie M. Anderson (35)

Trait_def: The technical stability and "grace under fire" a person maintains. It governs the ability to handle live mistakes, 16-hour filming days, or complex dialogue without breaking focus
Trait_rating_notes: Whereas Presence is a snapshot of professionalism and strength, Composure is the long-term ability to not only work hard, but succeed in grueling genres like anything that's live or films multiple times per week. Writers will typically lack this skill unless they also show signs of current or potential leadership (i.e.logistical command or creative cohesion)
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Tom Hanks (96), Keanu Reeves (88), Jennifer Lawrence (78), Shia LaBeouf (55), Lindsay Lohan (35), Anderson Cooper (98), Christiane Amanpour (89), Lester Holt (79), Don Lemon (65), Brian Williams (35), Kevin Feige (97), Shonda Rhimes (85), Ryan Murphy (75), Adam McKay (55), Charlie Kaufman (35)

Trait_def: The speed of mental processing and ability to navigate "off-script" moments. When they're being broadcast live, they don't sweat, in fact they shine! Covers ad-libbing for actors, improv skills for comedians, being a great talk show guest, and any sort of broadcasting a live event (whether it's the super bowl or Wipeout).
Trait_rating_notes: Anyone who is sharp as an interviewer or interviewee could score high here. It's not always abotu humor, but humor is an indicator. People who interact with fans in small or large numbers do well here. Behind the scenes people, even sucesful writers, producers, and showrunners will lack this skill unless otherwise demonstrated on camera.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Robin Williams (99), Ryan Reynolds (88), Paul Rudd (75), Vin Diesel (55), Steven Seagal (35), Anderson Cooper (97), Rachel Maddow (86), Lester Holt (72), Wolf Blitzer (55), Keith Olbermann (35), Conan O'Brien (98), Tina Fey (88), Aaron Sorkin (75), Kevin Feige (50), George R.R. Martin (35)

Trait_def: The level of visceral commitment and "stakes" a person brings to their work. The difference between a casual performance and one that feels vital, urgent, or dangerous
Trait_rating_notes: Can be seen in a humorous, dramatic, or real life (news) context. It's the sign of someone who's locked in and clearly performing their part, whatever it is, with conviction. Behind the scenes people, even sucesful writers, producers, and showrunners will lack this skill unless otherwise demonstrated on camera.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Viola Davis (96), Joaquin Phoenix (88), Adam Driver (75), Jennifer Aniston (55), Michael Cera (35), Christiane Amanpour (97), Anderson Cooper (85), Jake Tapper (78), Hoda Kotb (50), Al Roker (35), Werner Herzog (95), Quentin Tarantino (88), Greta Gerwig (72), Greg Daniels (50), Chuck Lorre (35)

Trait_def: The ability to inhabit and project complex internal states. For writers, it is the ability to craft multi-layered character arcs; for actors, it is the "inner life" that makes a performance feel human
Trait_rating_notes: Actors who portray a range of emotions do well here, even comedians who aren't seen as one-dimensional or merely 'sitcom' actors. This is the key area for a person to be defined as a succesful dramatic writer or actor. People in the news who have gravitas for serious coverage and have believable sincerity will do well here.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Olivia Colman (98), Daniel Day-Lewis (88), Adam Driver (78), Vin Diesel (55), Tommy Wiseau (35), Christiane Amanpour (96), Anderson Cooper (85), David Muir (75), Steve Doocy (50), Guy Fieri (35), Kenneth Lonergan (97), Phoebe Waller-Bridge (86), Taylor Sheridan (74), Michael Bay (45), Tommy Tallarico (35)

Trait_def: The mastery of rhythm, pacing, and the "weight" of a line. Technical command over silence and the flow of dialogue to maximize tension
Trait_rating_notes: This is almost entirely a trait for Actors. Comedic actors can be good at this unless they're strictly slapstick. Newspeople possess this skill potentially if they're delivery is known to be effectively clipped in manner or well-varied in tone or pace. A good newsperson doesn't need this skill, but a great one might have it. Even succesful drama writers won't necessarily possess an elite score here, but some are particularly known for the pacing of their scripts (Aaron Sorkin and Succession obviously).
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Anthony Hopkins (98), Viola Davis (88), Pedro Pascal (75), Seth Rogen (55), Carrot Top (35), Walter Cronkite (95), Anderson Cooper (85), Lester Holt (78), Wolf Blitzer (58), Evrod Cassimy (35), Aaron Sorkin (99), Jesse Armstrong (88), Shonda Rhimes (76), Judd Apatow (52), Julie M. Anderson (35)

Trait_def: The architectural understanding of why something is funny and being able to generative jokes and funny scenarios. For a writer, the ability to build a joke; for an actor, the "knowingness" of the irony
Trait_rating_notes: Even comedic actors will struggle at this criteria if they are not naturally funny, as reading a funny script well does not make one funny. Behind-the-camera wpeople will not likely possess the skill unless proven otherwise.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Steve Martin (99), Martin Short (88), Phoebe Waller-Bridge (78), Selena Gomez (55), Aaron Dominguez (35), Anderson Cooper (92), Megyn Kelly (82), Brian Williams (72), Wolf Blitzer (50), Shepard Smith (35), Larry David (98), Mike Judge (89), Shonda Rhimes (74), Judd Apatow (55), George R.R. Martin (35)

Trait_def: The mechanical precision of delivering a beat. The "internal clock" that knows exactly when to pause or deliver a punchline for maximum effect
Trait_rating_notes: Anyone who makes people laugh on TV could have a high score here, even if they aren't inately able to be funny in real life. Most people in news will lack this entirely, but some, like Anderson Cooper has proven to have a wry delivery, and morning news people often have a light humorous tone in less-serious segments. Even a hilarious comedy writer however will lack this skill unless they've demonstrated a stand-up career or ocasionall on-air spots.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Steve Martin (97), Julia Louis-Dreyfus (88), Ryan Reynolds (75), Jason Statham (55), Vin Diesel (35), Anderson Cooper (91), Hoda Kotb (82), Michael Strahan (72), Wolf Blitzer (50), David Muir (35), Tina Fey (96), Larry David (85), Mindy Kaling (74), Shonda Rhimes (45), Christopher Nolan (35)

Trait_def: The depth of subject-matter expertise. Determines the credibility of a sports analyst, a journalist’s research, or a reality contestant’s "Game IQ."
Trait_rating_notes: This will be most evident in news figures who are more than news 'readers' merely. People who are geniuses in one area or another (cooking, Football, Modeling, FInance, Military History, Current Politics, etc.). The area of genius is *not* in a genre of entertainment or anything else relating to acting; that ability is elsewhere in other ratings. People in reality genres, if they are a master of competitions, they might score well here. Anyone scoring above 'below average' or 'no ability', must have a tie to a specific area of expertise, not just intelligence in general.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Gordon Ramsay (96), Ken Jennings (85), Tony Romo (75), Kim Kardashian (55), Pauly D (35), Christiane Amanpour (98), Sanjay Gupta (88), John King (78), local lifestyle reporter (55), rookie traffic reporter (35), Kevin Feige (97), Anthony Bourdain (86), Michael Lewis (74), reality TV producer (50), background extra (35)

Trait_def: The professional mastery of unscripted delivery. Ability to read a prompter naturally, fill "dead air," and manage segment transitions seamlessly
Trait_rating_notes: This is a newsreading skill for anchors and reporters, also, but less so, for those in Live Sports. Without verfiied experience, this skill would be low for anyone else.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Neil Patrick Harris (96), Ryan Reynolds (88), Tom Hanks (78), Keanu Reeves (58), Joaquin Phoenix (35), Anderson Cooper (99), David Muir (89), Jake Tapper (79), Local traffic reporter (60), Rookie reporter (35), Jon Stewart (97), Tina Fey (88), Kevin Feige (75), Script writer (50), George R.R. Martin (35)

Trait_def: The ability to manage the "machine" of a show. Covers staying on budget, hitting deadlines, and navigating network resources
Trait_rating_notes: This is a management and leadership skill. Producers and Showrunners must have this to be successful. Only rarely would an on-camera person possess the skill unless verified to also be honing leadership and production skills.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Tom Cruise (95), George Clooney (85), Reese Witherspoon (78), Ashton Kutcher (55), Cillian Murphy (35), Roger Ailes (96), Lorne Michaels (88), Christiane Amanpour (75), local news anchor (50), rookie reporter (35), Kevin Feige (98), Kathleen Kennedy (89), Shonda Rhimes (78), independent producer (55), first-time screenwriter (35)

Trait_def: The ability to unify a creative vision across a team. The "Showrunner" skill of keeping a room on-voice and ensuring a singular, high-quality whole
Trait_rating_notes: This is a behind the scene ability to foster a creative environment and keep a show or movie from becoming stagnant. It would be entirely absent from sports and news, very uncommon in reality, and for scripted, sketch, and late night, almost only present in showrunners or head writers (if known for their wide influence), and almost never on-screen talent.
Trait_rating_scale: 90-100 is elite, 80-89 is great, 70-79 is good, 40-69 is below average to average, 35 is no discernable skill in this area
Trait_exemplars: Tom Cruise (96), George Clooney (84), Ben Affleck (75), Chris Evans (55), West Mulholland (35), Jon Stewart (94), Rachel Maddow (85), Anderson Cooper (78), Wolf Blitzer (50), Keith Olbermann (35), Vince Gilligan (99), Shonda Rhimes (87), Steven Soderbergh (79), Judd Apatow (55), Julie M. Anderson (35)
